## MARKET CONDITIONS & TRENDS

Average price per acre in Taos County: $23,529 based on 7,873 acres of land for sale.

Current median listing price for land in Taos County: $595,000.

LandWatch has 55 land listings in Tres Piedras specifically.

Tres Piedras Estates: No HOA, no covenants, no property owners association.

Ranchos de Taos Estates residents share common goals of off-grid living and unrestricted land use.

Seasonal patterns: Limited data on seasonal price changes. Owner financing is commonly offered in subdivisions like Tres Piedras and Carson Estates, particularly for smaller parcels under $5,000.

## ZOOK CABINS

**Current Pricing:**

Pricing starts around $132,500 for their Rockwood model
. Specific 2025-2026 pricing for Heritage, Grove ADU, and Rivara ADU models not publicly listed; direct quote required from dealer.

**Price Changes 2025-2026:**
No explicit price increase announcements found in current sources. 
All park models at Zook Cabins include cost of delivery, avoiding additional transport costs separate from home price
.

**Customer Reviews:**
Positive: 
Customers selected Zook based on great floor plans, innovative design features, and excellent customer reviews
. 
One customer reported "Matt at Zook cabins was extremely helpful even with many redesigns…the workmanship is outstanding and delivery and installation was incredible. Would highly recommend them!"

Multiple customers praised service and communication throughout the process
.

Negative: No significant negative reviews found in search results.

**New Mexico Delivery:**

Zook Cabins delivers modular Log and Modern Cabins all over the United States (with exception of Alaska and Hawaii)
. 
Documented delivery to Chama, NM
. 
Zook cabins are designed to provide dependable comfort across New Mexico's seasons with quality insulation and durable construction
.

**Models and Availability:**
Heritage, Grove ADU, and Rivara ADU all listed on current website. 
A-Frame Modern ADU offered in both 1-bedroom and 2-bedroom floor plans with more square footage than A-Frame Park Model
. 
2026 ADUs by Zook Cabins are modern designed single-wide certified modulars
.

## MIGHTY SMALL HOMES

**Current Pricing:**

Price estimate is $140-$328+ per square foot, with cost including kit, labor, and site work
. 
Freight approximately $4 per mile with minimum charge of $1,200 from Louisville, Kentucky
.

For specific models at 600-768 sf: A 768 sf ranch at mid-range would estimate $107,520-$252,224 before delivery; actual costs depend heavily on assembly method (DIY vs. contractor).

**Price Changes 2025-2026:**
No explicit price announcements found.

**Customer Reviews:**

Facebook page shows 92% recommend rating (10 reviews)
. 
Kits can be completed and shipped in 6 weeks, and in as little as 4 weeks if buyers make no customizations
.

Negative: 
Mighty Small Homes does not assemble the kits themselves; buyers must work with a local contractor or handle the build themselves with basic carpentry skills
. This increases complexity for off-grid Taos County build.

**New Mexico Delivery:**

Freight approximately $4 per mile with minimum $1,200 charge from Louisville, Kentucky
. Louisville to Taos County (~1,600 miles) = ~$7,600 freight. No specific New Mexico testimonials found in search results.

**Models:**
A-Frame 506sf, Modern 600sf, Cottage 600
sections.offgrid:
## NM Solar Incentives

**Federal Solar Tax Credit Eliminated (2026)** — 
President Trump signed legislation on July 4, 2025, which completely cancels the home solar tax credit beginning on January 1, 2026, 10 years before it was supposed to expire.
 https://solarsme.com/solarsme-new-mexico/

**State Solar Tax Credit Remains (10%, up to $6,000)** — 
Property owners can receive a 10% tax credit, up to $6,000; a NM taxpayer who has purchased and installed a solar thermal or solar photovoltaic system on or after March 1, 2025 is eligible.
 https://clean.energy.nm.gov/solar-panel-incentives/

**Proposed Battery Storage Tax Credit Failed (2025)** — 
House Bill 51 died in the House Taxation & Revenue Committee during the 2025 legislative session and was not enacted.
 https://exspenditure.com/solar/new-mexico-solar-incentives/

## Taos County Building & Zoning

**Land Use Regulations Modernization Underway** — 
Taos County has hired Architectural Research Consultants, Inc. to revise and update the Land Use Regulations (Ordinance 2018-2) and Subdivision Regulations (Ordinance 2005-8); by updating these regulations, Taos County hopes to modernize the current codes, simplify and make them more user-friendly, and encourage ecologically sound and sustainable development practices.
 https://www.taoscounty.org/492/Land-Use-and-Subdivision-Regulations-Upd

## Alternative Housing: Earthships

**Greater World Earthship Community Expanding** — 
On a sprawling mesa northwest of Taos sits the 600+ acre Greater World Earthship Community, started by Mike Reynolds and Earthship Biotecture; the community includes more than 300 acres of shared land and is fully off the grid, using exclusively solar and wind power.
 https://taos.org/discover/earthships-in-taos/

**Earthship Academy & Tourist Interest Growth** — 
More than 200,000 visitors check out the prototypes in Taos every year; nearly 200 students learn about Reynolds' principles every year at the Earthship Academy in a month-long course.
 https://reasonstobecheerful.world/earthships-taos-new-mexico-sustainable-construction/

**Earthship Documentary Released (2025)** — 
A YouTube film by Peter Santenello featuring an interview with Michael Reynolds, founder of the Earthship movement, was released Nov. 15, 2025.
 https://www.resilience.org/stories/2026-03-27/inside-the-off-grid-earthship-community-in-
